questions.question: Which Asian country released its ‘National Climate Research agenda’?
questions.explanation: India’s National Climate Research agenda was released during the inauguration of the 2-day International Climate Research Conclave. It was organised at Department of Science and Technology’s Centre of Excellence in Climate Studies at IIT Bombay. Its aim is to coordinate national efforts focusing on the understanding and addressing climate change by 2030 and beyond.

questions.question: Saudi Arabia announced to restore full diplomatic ties with which country?
questions.explanation: Saudi Arabia and Canada to restore full diplomatic ties and appoint new ambassadors, bringing an end to a dispute from 2018. The rift between the two countries began when Canada’s embassy in Riyadh published a tweet in Arabic, calling for the immediate release of women’s rights activists who were detained in Saudi Arabia.

questions.question: Which state is set to develop the Konark Sun Temple Complex?
questions.explanation: The Odisha government has recently decided to develop the Konark Sun Temple Complex with an outlay of Rs.209 crore. It will be developed as part of the Konark Heritage Area Development Plan (KHADP), under the Integrated Development of Heritage & Monuments and Tourist Destination Scheme.

questions.question: Which country has granted approval to include TOEFL test as a valid option for Student Direct Stream program?
questions.explanation: The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has granted approval to include TOEFL test scores as a valid option for the Student Direct Stream program in Canada. The Student Direct Stream (SDS) is a program designed to accelerate the processing of study permit applications for international students intending to enroll in designated learning institutions for post-secondary education in Canada.

questions.question: As of 2023, India has how many joint logistics nodes?
questions.explanation: The armed forces are planning to set up additional joint logistics nodes across the country to boost integration in their logistical needs and streamline their supply chains. While the last joint logistics node was operationalised in Mumbai in April 2021, the nodes in Guwahati and Tri-Services, Port Blair were operationalised in January 2021. The Joint Operations Division under the Headquarters Integrated Defence Staff has been responsible for establishing the nodes.

questions.question: Which institution notified ‘Indian Standards Related to AYUSH Herbs and Products’?
questions.explanation: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) recently notified 31 Indian standards related to Ayush herbs and products. It includes 30 herbs and one product including a stainless steel neti pot. An additional department was earlier established with a dedicated focus on Ayush at BIS.

questions.question: NTPC Green Energy Limited signed MoU with which state to collaborate in the development of Renewable Energy projects?
questions.explanation: An MoU was signed recently by the NTPC Green Energy Limited (NGEL) and Uttar Pradesh Rajya Vidyut Utpadan Nigam Limited (UPRVUNL) to collaborate in the development of Renewable Energy Parks and Projects. This MoU also seeks to facilitate renewable energy transition in India. NTPC is India’s largest Power Utility, with a total installed capacity of about 72 GW.

questions.question: 62 Desiccation-Tolerant Vascular Plant Species have been recently discovered in which country?
questions.explanation: As many as 62 Desiccation-Tolerant Vascular plant species have been discovered in the Western Ghats in India. These species have potential applications in agriculture, especially in regions where there is scarcity of water.

questions.question: Which country has developed new class of 3D-printed alloys that removed the need for aluminium and vanadium?
questions.explanation: Ti-6Al-4V is a titanium alloy that has 6% aluminium and 4% vanadium. It accounts for 50 percent of the entire titanium market. Scientists in Australia have created a new class of 3D-printed alloys that removed the need for aluminium and vanadium and makes use of oxygen and iron instead.

questions.question: Which institution released a report titled ‘Derailment in Indian Railways’?

options.label: C
options.text: Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG)

answer.label: C
answer.text: Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG)

questions.explanation: A report titled ‘Derailment in Indian Railways’ was released in 2022 by the Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG). This report flagged several shortcomings and provided multiple recommendations to improve the safety of the Indian Railways. It highlighted shortfalls in inspections and failures to use a dedicated railway fund on priority tasks.
